citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
An adult female shihtzu (#110, approximately 6-7 years old), randomly pulled for examination, had a moderate amount of
tartar built up on the left side of its mouth. The dog was moving/squirming a lot and was very difficult to examine. The
licensee had not observed the tartar buildup prior to inspection. Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection
which can become systemic. Daily observation must be done for all animals to assess their health and well-being and a
mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of
animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be
contacted and the dog must be treated as per his/her instructions. Correct by 5/29/26.

At the time of inspection there was no written exercise plan available for review despite dogs over 12 weeks of age being
present at the facility. Exercise for dogs is critical for healthy development and psychological well-being. The licensee
must develop, document, and follow an appropriate plan to provide dogs with the opportunity for exercise. In addition, the
plan must be approved by the attending veterinarian. The plan must include written standard procedures to be followed in
providing the opportunity for exercise. Correct by 6/27/26.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Many of the dog kennels had an accumulation of dog feces. When asked, the licensee stated it had been a couple of days
since the waste material had been removed in the main kennel and it had been a few days since the waste material had
been removed in the whelping building. Not removing waste material from the enclosures on a daily basis can attract
various types of pests and can contribute to the spread of disease. Waste material must be removed from the enclosures
on a daily basis. Correct starting on 5/29/26.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The main kennel has an accumulation of sheered dog hair on the floor. Empty bags of food and plastic trays were also
observed to be laying on the floor. An accumulation of dead flies were observed on the window sill. The cabinet in the
main kennel used to store husbandry products and medications has an accumulation of dust inside.
The floor of the whelping building had an accumulation of plastic bags and shredded paper scattered about on the
concrete flooring. The shredded paper and plastic garbage bags covered approximately 50% of the concrete floor.
Trash, junk, and waste products doesn't facilitate the cleaning of the premises, it can attract rodents, flies, and other
pests, and can contribute to the spread of disease. In addition, if dogs were to get loose in areas with trash, junk, and
waste products, they could be injured.
For protection of the health and well-being of the dogs, all premises must be kept free of accumulations of trash, junk,
waste products, and discarded matter so as to facilitate the cleaning of the premises and pest control.

The attending veterinarian has not visited the kennel since January 29th, 2025. Not having the attending veterinarian
conduct regularly scheduled visits to the kennel, not less than once every 12 months, doesn't allow the attending
veterinarian to assess and ensure the adequacy of veterinary care and other aspects of animal care. The licensee must
have the attending veterinarian visit the kennel and then have the attending veterinarian conduct regularly scheduled
visits not less than once every 12 months. Correct by 6/27/26.

A complete physical examination from head to tail of each dog has not been completed by the attending veterinarian
since January 29th, 2025. Not having the attending veterinarian conduct annual physical examination of the dogs in the
kennels doesn't ensure the health of the kennel. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and physical examinations
must be completed for all dogs in the kennel. Correct by 6/27/26.

No adult dogs in the kennel have been sampled for heartworm or fecal parasites in the past year. In addition, at least four
dogs are overdue on their distemper/parvo vaccinations and at least two dogs are overdue on their rabies vaccinations.
Not having the population sampled for the various parasites and not having all dogs up to date on their rabies and
distemper/parvo vaccinations doesn't ensure the population is adequately protected against the various
diseases/parasites that could infect them. The licensee must get the population sampled for heartworm and fecal
parasites in accordance with the directions from the program of veterinary care. In addition, the licensee must get all dogs
up to date on their rabies and distemper/parvo vaccinations. Correct by 6/27/26.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
End of report.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
An adult female shihtzu (#110, approximately 6-7 years old), randomly pulled for examination, had a moderate amount of 
tartar built up on the left side of its mouth. The dog was moving/squirming a lot and was very difficult to examine. The 
licensee had not observed the tartar buildup prior to inspection. Dental issues can be painful and can lead to infection 
which can become systemic. Daily observation must be done for all animals to assess their health and well-being and a 
mechanism of direct and frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of 
animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian. The attending veterinarian must be 
contacted and the dog must be treated as per his/her instructions. Correct by 5/29/26. 
3.8 
   
Exercise for dogs. 
At the time of inspection there was no written exercise plan available for review despite dogs over 12 weeks of age being 
present at the facility. Exercise for dogs is critical for healthy development and psychological well-being. The licensee 
must develop, document, and follow an appropriate plan to provide dogs with the opportunity for exercise. In addition, the 
plan must be approved by the attending veterinarian. The plan must include written standard procedures to be followed in 
providing the opportunity for exercise. Correct by 6/27/26. 
3.11(a) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
Many of the dog kennels had an accumulation of dog feces. When asked, the licensee stated it had been a couple of days 
since the waste material had been removed in the main kennel and it had been a few days since the waste material had 
been removed in the whelping building. Not removing waste material from the enclosures on a daily basis can attract 
various types of pests and can contribute to the spread of disease. Waste material must be removed from the enclosures 
on a daily basis. Correct starting on 5/29/26. 
3.11(c)  Repeat
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The main kennel has an accumulation of sheered dog hair on the floor. Empty bags of food and plastic trays were also 
observed to be laying on the floor. An accumulation of dead flies were observed on the window sill. The cabinet in the

main kennel used to store husbandry products and medications has an accumulation of dust inside. 
 
The floor of the whelping building had an accumulation of plastic bags and shredded paper scattered about on the 
concrete flooring. The shredded paper and plastic garbage bags covered approximately 50% of the concrete floor. 
 
Trash, junk, and waste products doesn't facilitate the cleaning of the premises, it can attract rodents, flies, and other 
pests, and can contribute to the spread of disease. In addition, if dogs were to get loose in areas with trash, junk, and 
waste products, they could be injured.  
 
For protection of the health and well-being of the dogs, all premises must be kept free of accumulations of trash, junk, 
waste products, and discarded matter so as to facilitate the cleaning of the premises and pest control. 
3.13(a)(1) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The attending veterinarian has not visited the kennel since January 29th, 2025. Not having the attending veterinarian 
conduct regularly scheduled visits to the kennel, not less than once every 12 months, doesn't allow the attending 
veterinarian to assess and ensure the adequacy of veterinary care and other aspects of animal care. The licensee must 
have the attending veterinarian visit the kennel and then have the attending veterinarian conduct regularly scheduled 
visits not less than once every 12 months. Correct by 6/27/26. 
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
A complete physical examination from head to tail of each dog has not been completed by the attending veterinarian 
since January 29th, 2025. Not having the attending veterinarian conduct annual physical examination of the dogs in the 
kennels doesn't ensure the health of the kennel. The attending veterinarian must be contacted and physical examinations 
must be completed for all dogs in the kennel. Correct by 6/27/26. 
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
No adult dogs in the kennel have been sampled for heartworm or fecal parasites in the past year. In addition, at least four 
dogs are overdue on their distemper/parvo vaccinations and at least two dogs are overdue on their rabies vaccinations. 
Not having the population sampled for the various parasites and not having all dogs up to date on their rabies and 
distemper/parvo vaccinations doesn't ensure the population is adequately protected against the various 
diseases/parasites that could infect them. The licensee must get the population sampled for heartworm and fecal 
parasites in accordance with the directions from the program of veterinary care. In addition, the licensee must get all dogs 
up to date on their rabies and distemper/parvo vaccinations. Correct by 6/27/26. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee. 
 
End of report.
